name: MESOBLASTIC NEPHROMA

demographics.Incidence: most common solid renal neoplasm discovered in 2nd / 3rd trimester, in neonate or in
demographics.Peak age: 1-3 months; 90% within 1st year of life; rare after the age of 6 months; may
demographics.Path: solid unencapsulated mass infiltrating renal parenchyma (derived from early
demographics.Histo: monomorphic tumor composed of uniform elongated spindle cells arranged in

dominant_finding: areas of low attenuation in large lesions ← hemorrhage / necrosis
distribution: often infiltrative tumor with extension into renal hilum / perinephric fat
discriminating_features: often infiltrative tumor with extension into renal hilum / perinephric fat
fluid-filled spaces ← hemorrhage, necrosis, cyst formation

general: often infiltrative tumor with extension into renal hilum / perinephric fat
fluid-filled spaces ← hemorrhage, necrosis, cyst formation
may produce multiple cystic spaces ← hemorrhage, necrosis
NO sharp cleavage plane toward normal parenchyma
involvement of renal hilum without invasion of vessels
large noncalcified renal mass with distortion of collecting system
usually NO herniation into renal pelvis (DDx: MLCN)
CECT: minimal predominantly peripheral enhancement ← functioning entrapped renal elements at
areas of low attenuation in large lesions ← hemorrhage / necrosis
MR: variable from markedly hypo- to hyperintense on T2WI
restricted diffusion in solid portion ← increased cellularity
focal areas of T1 shortening ← hemorrhage
US: evenly echogenic tumor resembling uterine fibroids
"ring" sign = concentric rings of alternating hyper- and hypoechogenicity surrounding tumor
complex heterogeneous mass with hemorrhage + cyst formation + necrosis
ANGIO: hypervascular mass with neovascularity + displacement of adjacent vessels

clinical.Cx: pre-term labor, premature rupture of membranes
